completed_tasks.name: Async foundation: tokio-util, config, trait redesign, TelegramAdapter
Core wiring: polling, dispatch, user queue, handler, runner
End-to-end Telegram bot smoke test
completed_tasks.progress: Bot fully functional: receives messages, dispatches, runs agent loop with tool use, streams edits to Telegram. Two streaming completion bugs fixed this session. Needs human smoke test to confirm cursor removal works.

remaining_tasks.name: Human smoke test — verify streaming completes cleanly (cursor removed)

blockers.description: Need human to run smoke test to confirm streaming completion fix works. Two bugs were fixed: (1) handler didn't drop agent before channels so cloned senders kept consumer alive, (2) SSE parser didn't exit on finish_reason — hung waiting for [DONE] that OpenRouter may never send.
blockers.workaround: Run RUST_LOG=debug cargo run -- gateway, send a message, verify cursor disappears after response completes

human_actions_pending.action: Run 'RUST_LOG=debug cargo run -- gateway', send a Telegram message, verify the cursor disappears when response finishes
human_actions_pending.context: Two streaming bugs were fixed — need to confirm the fix works end-to-end

decisions.rationale: Cooperative shutdown propagates to all subtasks cleanly

decisions.decision: dispatch_future must run in tokio::select! with shutdown signal
decisions.rationale: Bug fix: was defined but only awaited after ctrl+c, so dispatch never ran

decisions.decision: Handler must use config.resolve_api_key() not config.model.api_key
decisions.rationale: Bug fix: env var resolution was bypassed, giving empty API key
agent.run(&self) borrows — agent stays alive, its callback closures hold cloned channel senders, preventing consumer from receiving None

decisions.decision: SSE parser returns immediately on finish_reason
decisions.rationale: OpenRouter may not send [DONE] marker and keeps connection alive; finish_reason is authoritative signal that stream is complete

decisions.decision: 60s chunk timeout on SSE byte_stream reads
decisions.rationale: Safety net — if stream stalls without sending finish_reason or closing, eventual timeout prevents indefinite hang

decisions.decision: Process usage before finish_reason in SSE parser
decisions.rationale: Some providers send usage in same chunk as finish_reason; early return on finish_reason would miss it otherwise

next_action: Run RUST_LOG=debug cargo run -- gateway, send a message via Telegram, confirm the cursor disappears after the response completes. If it works, commit the fixes and mark Phase 02 complete. If it still hangs, add more debug logging around flush(true) in stream_consumer.rs to trace whether the final edit is actually sent.
context_notes: All 5 plans are code-complete with 55 tests passing. The streaming hang was a two-layer bug. Layer 1: handler dropped channels but not the agent — since run(&self) borrows, the agent's callback closures held cloned senders alive, so the consumer channel never closed. Layer 2: SSE parser sent Done on finish_reason but continued looping waiting for [DONE] — OpenRouter keeps connections alive without [DONE], so byte_stream.next() hung forever, keeping tx alive, so the agent loop's rx.recv() also hung. Both are now fixed but untested.
